Yeah, so you should be able to select the status.
Oh, and the status.
Okay.
If you're unable to do that, then you're...

I just didn't try that.

to change the permissions for you.
sure it'll work.

Did all the fields look good?
Not yet.
I'll take a look at it right after this call.
Okay, okay, perfect.

For the first few of them, let's start.
We put in staging, so I can review and then we can publish, at least for the first three to five, and then that's going to take over completely.
Okay, okay, that sounds good.

So we've done some additional keyword research for you, we've generated new assignments.
Sydney can walk you through kind of the specific assignments that we've created.
Sydney, if you want, you can share your screen or I can go.
You're on mute.
Yeah, I realized right after I started talking.
Yeah, you can just share your screen, it's just in the content OS.
So if you remember when we presented our strategy in the first week, one of the campaigns that we wanted to go for is gun detection.
So, sorry, Aida, can you open the topic clusters?
Mm-hmm.
So these are the three topic clusters that we identified as.
as.

They are lumped together by topic cluster, yes.
But, yes, all of these are lumped together by, but, all the, sorry, the ideas in here are mostly just around the gun detection campaign that we're trying to push forward.
Okay, I guess, what's the difference between the tab assignments and the tab topic clusters?

the top, oh.
No, go ahead.
Sorry, didn't answer that quickly.

Um, yeah, so, basically what you said, so the GUN campaign covers all of them, um, so that,

Okay.
Depending on the nature of the blog, it fits into different topic clusters.
Okay.
Yeah.
Sorry.
Actually, Aida, can I share my screen?
Yeah.
Give me five seconds.
It might be a little bit clearer if I just show you the campaign.
I think it's this one.
This one.

Yeah.
Yeah.
Yeah.
So basically every week, and all these are in the content OS.

Solutions bucket, but all of the keywords in this list and currently in your content OS are related to gun detection.

Go ahead.
We don't really need to follow the sequence.

So, like, week one would be foundation problem agitation.
Week one and two, week three would be AI technology.
So this would talk about how guns work.
We don't need to follow this strictly.
I mostly did it because I want to make sure we cover all of these topics.
Like we don't need to cover all AI technology in week three, if that makes sense.
Do you care about how we come out with these different blogs?
Yeah, because I think the timing aspect of stuff is secondary.
Because we're trying to kind of push all of them just as fast as possible.
But not just as fast as possible.
It's how the search engines kind of just process information over time.
I think if we are thinking of it like the reader follows our sequence, therefore we take it from step one, problem, to step two, like...
I think that the model is kind of gone, but there's just too much information out there.
People will read stuff without necessarily following an episodic sequence.
See what I mean?
So I'm kind of like less concerned about the fact that you talk about the solution first, maybe, and the problem second, sequentially.
You want to talk about both, eventually, and relatively quickly.
And then just the information is out there, and then you work on distribution.
So, I mean, at least that's how I'm interpreting it.
I don't if you guys agree.
Yeah, no, that's exactly how it is.
I just only have it by the week because I wanted to make sure that we're covering all of those important topics.
But the actual publishing cadence, like what you said, readers aren't going to read all.
The articles and readers aren't going to read it sequentially, so it really, we should cover what you think is most important first.
Right.

In fact, actually, I would prioritize articles that cover more impactful keywords or search types in the LLMs, even if, from our point of view, maybe it's the last article in a, you know, logical step.
Yeah.
All that makes sense.

I'm just, so this is more for us to decide, so I don't want to, I don't know if you want to discuss this here, but like in terms of us literally just reviewing this list, which has many, many blogs to review, I guess perhaps what I was thinking is we should divide different topic clusters, so we're reviewing different things, like not, like kind of coordinating so that we cover more ground.
We're going to have more blogs ready to go.
We're
So we can talk about it.
Yeah, we should talk about it.
But I don't think they need to care.
What I think we need to be doing is giving you the information that you need to start executing at the right timing.

Okay.

Yeah, for sure, for sure.

I think this, but I think this, so it's good we're going to have to be faster in our review of the content.
I think we have a reference point with the one that we just did, and I think we've done a few at this point, even without the growthx team to know what we want and what good is.
So, yeah, let's divide and conquer.
Okay.
Because this...

Okay, great.
All right, perfect.

So if we notice that we're like running short on ideas, we will definitely reach out to get your eyes on our suggestions.
Okay, perfect.
So Sydney's going to shoot over the new blogs to you.
I wanted to share, to like walk you through your Looker dashboard.
So let me share my screen really quickly.
So you all should have access to it.
I'm going to send you the link in the chat as well, but there's also, the link is also in the agenda.
So your Looker dashboard, it includes data from your Google search console.
you for now.
You're
NGA4, and it's designed to primarily just help track your organic search performance, but we also focus on LLM visibility as well.
So the first page here kind of tracks your overall sessions versus your engaged sessions, and this helps you understand, like, the percentage of users on your site who don't bounce.

So an engaged session in Google, like, Google Analytics is a session that, Sydney, correct me if I'm wrong, is lasts longer than 10 seconds and includes or includes a page scroll or the user clicks on a link on that site to go to a different, like, internal link.
It's the opposite of a bounce rate, basically.
So, for example, your engagement rate for your homepage is close to 55%, which means that 50% of users come...
To your website and they leave very quickly without like spending more than 10 seconds on it.

Then the non-branded report includes data about landing pages that rank for keywords that don't include your brand name.
So this analyzes really your organic search performance for, you know, primarily like keywords that don't include your name and are more of an SEO play.
And then

Obviously, this depends on the conversions that you have set up in GA4.

Yeah, yeah, got it.
Yeah, this is, I think whoever set up the analytics probably has the best answer, but you can also set up new events that we can also pull into this.
You said, has anyone ever set it up?
I think it was Raj, and it's pretty much phenomenal, hope this is, okay, I'm not surprised.
So there's probably not, we probably haven't really set it up, it was stuff here and there, but yeah, there's work to be done there on our end.
Okay, okay, okay, it.
And then the cohort page, this one focuses on, right now we have

So here you're going to see different topic clusters.
So the topic clusters that we have set up in Airtable, they're also important for Looker because Looker is linked to Airtable.
And then we're going to be able to see not just which blog posts are performing really well, but also which clusters are performing really well.
And that helps us kind of understand like where to optimize and where to put more of our effort into.
But right now, this is just a fairly empty report.
And this is just a standard landing page report that kind of gives.
And you a visual overview of the data.
The queries report is built using your Google Search Console data.
So Google Search Console shows data about the keywords or the queries that bring users to your website.
So this is basically the keywords that you rank for, but only the ones that are collected by Google.
So this kind of gives a good overview of, you know, where you have the most visibility.
And it's also a good way to find ideas for new blog posts.
For example, one of my, like my very first client at GrowthX, they are a very new dental staffing app.
And we noticed that they were getting a lot of organic traffic for a query that consisted of their company name and reviews.
Which meant.

That a lot of people were just trying to find reviews about their app and like how, you know, what the experience was like, you know, using that app and their services, but they didn't have anything on their website to point those users to.
So we created a blog post that was just a combination of different, like actual user reviews from the app store and like the Google Play store.
And we just combined them into a nice blog post and that one ended up ranking on in the first position for that keyword and it's getting them a lot of leads to this day.
So it's, you know, it's also a good way to find blog ideas.
Where is the volume of searches in this?
Volume is, that would be, we don't have, we don't have volume.
Like Google doesn't share that, but the impressions, I think impressions are kind of maybe the closest to what you would be interested.
Impression is the number of times your, like a user sees your website in search results.
So if it appears on page two, for example, and a user scrolls to page two of Google search results, they might see your website, but they don't always click on it.
So you will always have more impressions than clicks.
It's kind of surprising.
This would say, if I look at that first row, Ambient.ai, we are, so second, we're at 44, right?
Yeah.
No, no, sorry.
The average position is like number one, your number Oh, okay.
So what's the landing page?
That would be, hold on.
It's the number of landing pages ranking for your keyword.
Yeah, yeah.
It's the number of landing.
pages in our website?
Yeah, ranking for that specific keyword.
We have 44 pages?
I think we have more than that, but we have 44 of them ranking specifically for Ambient.
Okay.
Yeah, it's ranking, like, across, like, 100 pages of Google search results.
So it's not, it is, it seems a lot, like, 44, but I think the Google search results go up to 100, like, pages.
Is that a good thing, that we have 44 ranking for that, or bad thing?
That's a good thing.
Yeah.
Yeah, you want to show up as many times as possible for your brand keyword.
Yeah, they are, that I understand, but that's more like the average position, but is it okay to have many pages like that to rank for the same keyword?
Is there, like, potential for cannibalization or stuff like that, or it doesn't matter?
Yeah.
cannibalization is more of a concern with non-brand keywords.
And, and, and, and, and, and, and
So like blog posts, but when you're ranking that highly, you're ranking basically in position one.
It's not a big deal.
Cannibalization is an issue, for example, let's say you have two pages that are ranking for similar keywords, but none of those keywords are on page one.
So you're like in page three, page four.
That's when cannibalization is an issue.
And we recommend removing one of those pages and like redirecting it to just one page and like improving the content so that that one can rank on page one, actually.
So cannibalization is an issue when you're not seeing like high rankings for none of the pages that are kind of competing for the same keyword.
Okay, got it.
All right.
And just to show you really quickly the LLM report, this one pulls data from your Google.
Analytics Dashboard, and it analyzes traffic that's sent to your site from different LLMs.
So you can see that like your biggest LLM referral is ChatGPT followed.
On most days, it seems to be perplexity, but you're also getting some referrals from Claude AI.
And this is a nice, so we have a scrunch dashboard.
Last week, we shared the scrunch dashboard with you.
And this report is a good, like, complementary tool to scrunch, because this is actual data from your website and that we can verify.
And scrunch is more, you know, we're trying to analyze the prompts that your audience could be using and then analyzing those responses.
But this is, you know, so you get two sides of that data here.
And then this report also contains, like, the different...

Page refers, so, and it also should contain, I'm not sure why it's not, okay, yeah, it just didn't want to scroll, and you're also going to see the different landing pages that are, that most commonly show up in AI responses, so obviously your homepage is at the top, one of your features, Threat Detection and Response, has also been getting cited by LLMs, so this is also a good way, once we start publishing blog content, we're going to start seeing blogs show up here as well, as LLMs start picking it up, like, for example, one of your blogs, like, like the feature highlights, or the glossary, like, what is Intelligent Video Analytics, this is already being cited by LLMs, and the more we publish, we're going to be seeing a lot more of the blog content here as well.
Great, how do we know if this is good or bad?
It's not an absolute value, but it's...
Is it high?
Is it low?
That's the next question that I'm going to get at Easter or by the board.
Yeah, yeah, I get it.
Get it totally.
I think, I mean, this would require us to really set a benchmark for what is, you know, we want to make sure that it's higher, that it's increasing.
What I'm seeing is, like, I would like to see a lot more traffic to the blog, the solutions, the features.
We want to get those numbers up.
I mean, this is okay, but I think it could be a lot, it could be a lot higher for, you know, this period of, this period of time.
Um, this is quite, like, this is January 1st, this is from the beginning of the year through September.
Um, so we could find a way to compare, probably, against, uh, some...
Either benchmarks or competitors, because, you know, I'm going to need to kind of somewhat quantify it.
I think the general trend of us showing improvement over time, good.
That's really one thing.
But they will want to know immediately, like, okay, is UI doing better than us or this other company?
I guarantee it, so.
Yeah, yeah, yeah, no, no, no, I get it.
Thinking through.
Yeah, yeah, for sure.
I mean, I think this is where Scrumge data might be more helpful, because Scrumge allows us to actually see your competitors and, like, let me actually, I'll share the report.
I mean, assuming our question will happen with the other keywords, too.
Like, when we have the gun detection campaign, and one of the key objectives to say we're improving our SEO.
Oh, we're
So we can show the absolute position on average where we land, but we should also, and if there is improvement, like we went from three to two, then we will want to also speak to the competitors.
Yeah, yeah, absolutely.
I mean, and this is something that we can also, like, when it comes to position tracking, SEMrush allows us to compare, like, your organic search rankings during a specific period of time.
So, and it allows us to, like, download the report so we can easily share it with you and you can distribute it internally.
Awesome.
Thank you.
All right, cool.
This is cool.
Perfect.
All right.
Well, we're here if you have any other questions, but we'll be shooting over the blogs to you shortly.
Awesome.
Thank you.
Thank you.
See you next week.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Weekly sync to discuss content strategy, review blog ideas, and analyze performance metrics for Ambient.ai's content marketing efforts.](https://fathom.video/share/nEvLyLAZxDFsLdJUrJDX1scLmzJsA2yF?tab=summary&timestamp=2.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [New blog ideas added to Content OS, focusing on gun detection campaign across multiple topic clusters](https://fathom.video/share/nEvLyLAZxDFsLdJUrJDX1scLmzJsA2yF?tab=summary&timestamp=130.0)
  - [Looker dashboard set up with Google Search Console and GA4 data to track organic search and LLM visibility performance](https://fathom.video/share/nEvLyLAZxDFsLdJUrJDX1scLmzJsA2yF?tab=summary&timestamp=835.0)
  - [Content approval process clarified: team to review and change status from "idea" to "approved" in Content OS](https://fathom.video/share/nEvLyLAZxDFsLdJUrJDX1scLmzJsA2yF?tab=summary&timestamp=706.0)
  - [Need to improve GA4 event tracking for more meaningful conversion data](https://fathom.video/share/nEvLyLAZxDFsLdJUrJDX1scLmzJsA2yF?tab=summary&timestamp=1035.0)

## Topics

### Content Strategy and Blog Ideas

  - [Gun detection campaign spans multiple topic clusters (AI threat detection, industry-specific solutions, etc.)](https://fathom.video/share/nEvLyLAZxDFsLdJUrJDX1scLmzJsA2yF?tab=summary&timestamp=161.0)
  - [New blog ideas added to Content OS, ready for review and approval](https://fathom.video/share/nEvLyLAZxDFsLdJUrJDX1scLmzJsA2yF?tab=summary&timestamp=130.0)
  - [Team to divide and conquer blog idea reviews, focusing on different topic clusters](https://fathom.video/share/nEvLyLAZxDFsLdJUrJDX1scLmzJsA2yF?tab=summary&timestamp=634.0)
  - [Prioritize articles with high-impact keywords, regardless of logical sequence](https://fathom.video/share/nEvLyLAZxDFsLdJUrJDX1scLmzJsA2yF?tab=summary&timestamp=501.0)

### Content Approval Process

  - [Team to review blog ideas in Content OS and change status from "idea" to "approved"](https://fathom.video/share/nEvLyLAZxDFsLdJUrJDX1scLmzJsA2yF?tab=summary&timestamp=706.0)
  - [GrowthX team will proactively reach out if running low on approved ideas](https://fathom.video/share/nEvLyLAZxDFsLdJUrJDX1scLmzJsA2yF?tab=summary&timestamp=798.0)
  - [Aim for faster review and approval process outside of weekly meetings](https://fathom.video/share/nEvLyLAZxDFsLdJUrJDX1scLmzJsA2yF?tab=summary&timestamp=720.0)

### Looker Dashboard Overview

  - [Tracks organic search performance and LLM visibility](https://fathom.video/share/nEvLyLAZxDFsLdJUrJDX1scLmzJsA2yF?tab=summary&timestamp=835.0)
  - [Key metrics: overall sessions vs. engaged sessions, non-branded keyword performance, conversion events](https://fathom.video/share/nEvLyLAZxDFsLdJUrJDX1scLmzJsA2yF?tab=summary&timestamp=866.0)
  - [Cohort page will show performance by topic cluster once more content is published](https://fathom.video/share/nEvLyLAZxDFsLdJUrJDX1scLmzJsA2yF?tab=summary&timestamp=1097.0)
  - [Queries report shows keywords driving traffic to the website](https://fathom.video/share/nEvLyLAZxDFsLdJUrJDX1scLmzJsA2yF?tab=summary&timestamp=1143.0)

### LLM Visibility and Performance

  - [ChatGPT is the largest LLM referral source, followed by Perplexity and Claude AI](https://fathom.video/share/nEvLyLAZxDFsLdJUrJDX1scLmzJsA2yF?tab=summary&timestamp=1450.0)
  - [Homepage and some feature pages (e.g., Threat Detection and Response) are being cited by LLMs](https://fathom.video/share/nEvLyLAZxDFsLdJUrJDX1scLmzJsA2yF?tab=summary&timestamp=1463.0)
  - [Team to work on establishing benchmarks and competitor comparisons for LLM performance](https://fathom.video/share/nEvLyLAZxDFsLdJUrJDX1scLmzJsA2yF?tab=summary&timestamp=1568.0)

### Google Analytics Setup

  - [Current GA4 events are generic (e.g., Page View, Session Start)](https://fathom.video/share/nEvLyLAZxDFsLdJUrJDX1scLmzJsA2yF?tab=summary&timestamp=1035.0)
  - [Need to improve event tracking for more meaningful conversion data](https://fathom.video/share/nEvLyLAZxDFsLdJUrJDX1scLmzJsA2yF?tab=summary&timestamp=1035.0)
  - ["Demo CTA" identified as the most important lead generation event](https://fathom.video/share/nEvLyLAZxDFsLdJUrJDX1scLmzJsA2yF?tab=summary&timestamp=1007.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Team to review and approve blog ideas in Content OS, dividing work by topic clusters](https://fathom.video/share/nEvLyLAZxDFsLdJUrJDX1scLmzJsA2yF?tab=summary&timestamp=634.0)
  - [Improve GA4 event tracking for more specific conversion data](https://fathom.video/share/nEvLyLAZxDFsLdJUrJDX1scLmzJsA2yF?tab=summary&timestamp=1035.0)
  - [Establish benchmarks and competitor comparisons for LLM performance metrics](https://fathom.video/share/nEvLyLAZxDFsLdJUrJDX1scLmzJsA2yF?tab=summary&timestamp=1568.0)
  - [GrowthX team to send over new blog content for review](https://fathom.video/share/nEvLyLAZxDFsLdJUrJDX1scLmzJsA2yF?tab=summary&timestamp=1727.0)
  - [Continue monitoring Looker dashboard and adjust content strategy based on performance data](https://fathom.video/share/nEvLyLAZxDFsLdJUrJDX1scLmzJsA2yF?tab=summary&timestamp=1723.0)

action_items.description: Review calibration block staged in CMS, approve for publishing
Coordinate with team to divide topic clusters for review, ensure coverage of all ideas
Review & approve blog ideas in content OS, change status from "idea" to "approved"
Send new blog ideas to Everest for review
Investigate GA4 event names, clarify meanings (e.g., Form Start, file download)
